commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bb7148c52f83f40b27a56cc4bbdb8b7bcc3e283b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds first-class Xiaomi Token Plan provider support with regional onboarding choices, auth/key handling, static model catalog/costs, usage/config wiring, tests, and Xiaomi provider docs updates.

PR surface: Source +563, Tests +285, Docs +75. Total +923 across 21 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the region gate accepts any provider object and key validation only rejects the opposite prefix. I did not execute tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor current-head proof and maintainer approval for the new provider/auth surface; automation cannot supply the contributor's live Xiaomi setup proof.

target.title: feat:Add Xiaomi Token Plan provider support
target.body:
## Summary

- add first-class `xiaomi-token-plan` support to the bundled Xiaomi provider plugin
- add regional Token Plan onboarding/auth choices (China/Europe/Singapore), static v2.5 catalog entries, and `tp-...` / `sk-...` key-shape validation
- wire the new provider through usage/auth/config overlay surfaces and update Xiaomi provider docs
- set model costs with tiered cache-read pricing: flat input/output per 1M tokens, cache-read pricing steps at 256K input tokens
- use model catalog names as display aliases instead of generic provider labels

## Why

OpenClaw's bundled Xiaomi plugin only supported pay-as-you-go `xiaomi` setup, while issue #86169 asked for Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan support without requiring manual custom-provider wiring.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xiaomi/index.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/onboard.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice-options.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts` — 7 files, 165 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/xiaomi/` — format clean
- `git diff --check` — clean

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw can onboard and resolve Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan as a first-class provider with regional preset endpoints instead of requiring manual custom-provider wiring.

Real environment tested: macOS local source checkout with live Xiaomi Token Plan China (`cn`) region `tp-...` API key. Direct `/v1/models` and `/v1/chat/completions` API calls verified against `https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1`. Full openclaw embedded agent integration tested through the `xiaomi-token-plan` provider for both `mimo-v2.5` and `mimo-v2.5-pro` models. Non-interactive `openclaw onboard --auth-choice xiaomi-token-plan-cn --xiaomi-token-plan-api-key "tp-..." --non-interactive --accept-risk` confirmed working end-to-end.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `curl -s "https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1/models"` — HTTP 200, returned 8 models including mimo-v2.5 and mimo-v2.5-pro.
2. `curl -s "https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1/chat/completions"` with `mimo-v2.5-pro` — HTTP 200, valid reasoning response with `reasoning_content`.
3. `curl -s "https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1/chat/completions"` with `mimo-v2.5` — HTTP 200, returned "hello world".
4. `openclaw agent --local --agent main --model "xiaomi-token-plan/mimo-v2.5" --message "Reply with exactly: hello from token plan" --thinking off --json` — `text: "hello from token plan"`, provider `xiaomi-token-plan`, no fallback.
5. `openclaw agent --local --agent main --model "xiaomi-token-plan/mimo-v2.5-pro" --message "What is 2+2? Answer in one word." --thinking off --json` — `text: "Four"`, provider `xiaomi-token-plan`, no fallback.
6. `openclaw onboard --non-interactive --accept-risk --auth-choice xiaomi-token-plan-cn --xiaomi-token-plan-api-key "tp-..." --json` — config written correctly with `baseUrl: "https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1"`, model aliases, and auth profile stored.

Evidence after fix: `xiaomi-token-plan` provider resolves correctly through the auth profile store, catalogs both Token Plan models (mimo-v2.5-pro, mimo-v2.5) with real costs, makes successful /v1/chat/completions calls with reasoning and non-reasoning models, and the embedded agent correctly routes `xiaomi-token-plan/model` references without fallback. Usage tracking captures input/output/cacheRead token counts with per-token cost calculation including tiered cache-read pricing. The `openclaw onboard --auth-choice xiaomi-token-plan-cn` flow works end-to-end in non-interactive mode.

Observed result after fix: All 6 live API/agent/onboard integration tests passed end-to-end. Provider correctly resolves `cn` region base URL, authenticates with `tp-...` key, and returns valid chat completions with working prompt caching (observed cache hits in usage data). Model dropdown shows specific names ("Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro") instead of the generic provider label.

### Model costs

| Model | Input | Output | cacheRead (<256K) | cacheRead (>=256K) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `mimo-v2.5-pro` | $1/1M | $3/1M | $0.20/1M | $0.40/1M |
| `mimo-v2.5` | $0.4/1M | $2/1M | $0.08/1M | $0.16/1M |

Prices are USD per million tokens. Cache-read pricing uses tieredPricing with a step at 256,000 input tokens.

What was not tested: The `ams` (Europe) and `sgp` (Singapore) regional endpoints, real-time streaming behavior, multi-turn conversations with the Token Plan provider, GitHub Actions / Crabbox / Testbox CI runs.

Fixes #86169.
target.author: nianjiuzst
